Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and low total CO₂, suggesting impaired renal function and possible metabolic acidosis. Marked proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support significant renal pathology, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. Elevated alkaline phosphatase may indicate hepatic or bone involvement. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.
